z3.claim: Given computed O associator coefficients, asserting all basis-triple associators are zero is UNSAT; erasing that zero constraint is SAT.

notes: Julia is authoritative for H via CliffordAlgebras and O via Cayley-Dickson over that package H table.
JAX supplies the dual-SMT structural proof over computed coefficients.
PyTorch supplies the torch.func.jacrev associator-energy sensitivity, not exact algebra authority.

process_snapshot.note: Process state is not durable truth; artifact and validator states are separated from live worker closure.

high: R0 VERDICT=DECORATIVE LINE=121 REASON=The SMT flip is built from RealVal(pa) != RealVal(pb), so the UNSAT side reduces to const-vs-const Born-probability comparisons already computed outside the solver.

F01 VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=100 REASON=The UNSAT side asserts r <= d over solver integers bound to computed carrier dimension and eigvalsh rank, and dropping that bound flips the over-support candidate to SAT.
N01 VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=152 REASON=The UNSAT side binds B to the JAX-computed X matrix entries before asserting zero commutator equations, while the erased case leaves B free and becomes SAT.
R2adm VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=77 REASON=The UNSAT side is the PSD determinant inequality over solver reals pinned to computed matrix entries, and dropping psd admits the same candidate.
R2quot VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=213 REASON=The UNSAT side asks for a stability violation over solver real expectation variables bound to computed Tr(rho O) tables, not prebound boolean relation facts.
low: R0 VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=124 REASON=SMT asserts existence of a separator as disequalities over JAX-computed Born probabilities, so Z-only UNSAT is from computed equal probabilities rather than a free Boolean contradiction.
F01 VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=100 REASON=The dropped F01 constraint is the arithmetic bound r <= d over solver integers pinned to JAX-computed carrier dimension and eigvalsh rank.
N01 VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=153 REASON=The UNSAT claim asserts zero commutator equations over solver matrix entries pinned to JAX-computed Pauli Z and X integer entries.
R2adm VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=77 REASON=The dropped PSD constraint is determinant/nonnegativity arithmetic over solver reals pinned to JAX-computed density-matrix entries.

R2quot VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=213 REASON=The UNSAT stability check is an OR of violation predicates built from Real equalities over variables pinned to JAX-computed expectation tables, not precomputed relation booleans.
medium: R0 VERDICT=DECORATIVE LINE=121 REASON=The solver is fed direct RealVal-vs-RealVal Born-probability comparisons, so the UNSAT/SAT flip reduces to ground constant equalities rather than variables tied inside SMT.
F01 VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=100 REASON=The flip depends on the arithmetic contradiction r=4, d=3, and r<=d with r and d bound to JAX-computed spectral rank and carrier dimension.

N01 VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=152 REASON=The dropped X-matrix binding makes the zero-commutator equations SAT, while with JAX-computed Z and X matrix entries the commutator-zero constraints are arithmetically UNSAT.
R2adm VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=77 REASON=Dropping PSD removes the determinant inequality over solver variables pinned to JAX-computed matrix entries, flipping the non-PSD candidate from UNSAT to SAT.
R2quot VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=213 REASON=The UNSAT no-violation check is over solver Real expectation variables pinned to JAX-computed Tr(rho O) values, not a precomputed relation Bool matrix.
xhigh: R0 VERDICT=DECORATIVE LINE=121 REASON=The solver is fed direct RealVal(pa) != RealVal(pb) constant comparisons from computed Born probabilities, so the UNSAT reduces to a ground literal.
F01 VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=100 REASON=The dropped F01 constraint r <= d is applied to Int vars pinned to computed carrier dimension and eigvalsh rank, making over_r=4 contradict d=3.
N01 VERDICT=GENUINE LINE=152 REASON=The erased case drops the computed X-matrix binding, and with it present the zero-commutator equations over Z/X matrix-entry vars are UNSAT.
